source_note: Segment AUM below is a bottom-up sum of the 43 raw institution records in ie_institutions_latest.json (results (5)-(8).xls), classified against Atlas's entity_type/sub_type fields rather than a raw S&P Category column (Ireland's export didn't carry that field the way the UK one did, so this mapping is less granular than UK's 19-segment split). Two things flagged for review rather than silently decided: (1) National Treasury Management Agency is recorded with entity_type 'Other', but one of its plans is explicitly typed 'Sovereign Wealth Fund' in the underlying export (matches reality — NTMA manages the Ireland Strategic Investment Fund) — classified here as SWF rather than left in an Other/unclassified bucket. (2) Eight commercial state bodies (An Post, Bord Na Mona, Coillte, Dublin Port Company, EirGrid, ESB, Ervia, Irish Aviation Authority — EUR 6.358bn combined) are currently sitting in DB Pension (Corp) because their sub_type wasn't marked 'Public Sector' the way Coras Iompair Eireann and Courts Service of Ireland were. Whether Ireland's semi-state sector should count as Govt or Corp for this matrix is a judgement call, not something to auto-decide — move them to DB Pension (Govt) if that's the right call. No DC pension entities appeared in this export at all (all 34 pension records are DB). Coverage note: 43 institutions, EUR 62.501bn tracked, no independent check yet on how exhaustive that is against Ireland's actual DB/E&F/SWF universe.
scorecard_note: Not yet scored — none of the 12 opportunity-scorecard dimensions have been filled in for any Ireland segment. Sign in and use the matrix below to start scoring; see Sources & Methodology for the scoring convention and Overall formula.
allocation_note: Not yet built. The UK allocation breakdown was derived from the S&P allocation export's plan-level detail scaled against a validated equity figure — Ireland's raw institution records do carry plan-level allocation lines (see ie_institutions_latest.json), so the same approach is possible, but the equity validation step and the scaling work haven't been done yet.
